blocks:
Surah Fussilat

Makkan, and it is 54 verses

Surah Ha Mim As-Sajdah

Makkan, the number of its verses is fifty-three verses بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) حم. (1) Ha Mim. (٢) تَنْزِيلٌ مِنَ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ. (2) A sending down from the All-beneficent, the All-merciful. (٣) كِتابٌ فُصِّلَتْ آياتُهُ ** (3) A Book whose signs have been articulated Al-Qummi said: meaning, its lawful (حلالها), its unlawful (حرامها), its rulings (أحكامها), and its practices (سننها) have been made clear. قُرْآناً عَرَبِيًّا لِقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ. as an Arabic Quran for a people who know. (٤) بَشِيراً وَنَذِيراً. (4) as a bearer of good news and a warner. Al-Qummi said: It gives good news to the believers and warns the wrongdoers (الظالمين). فَأَعْرَضَ أَكْثَرُهُمْ ** But most of them turned away, from reflecting upon it and accepting it. فَهُمْ لا يَسْمَعُونَ ** so they do not hear, a hearing of contemplation and obedience. (٥) وَقالُوا قُلُوبُنا فِي أَكِنَّةٍ مِمَّا تَدْعُونا إِلَيْهِ ** (5) And they say, 'Our hearts are in wraps against that to which you invite us, meaning, in coverings (أغطية). وَفِي آذانِنا وَقْرٌ ** and there is a deafness in our ears, meaning, deafness (صمم), the origin of which is heaviness (الثقل). وَمِنْ بَيْنِنا وَبَيْنِكَ حِجابٌ ** and there is a curtain between us and you. which prevents us from communication (التواصل). Al-Qummi said: meaning, you invite us to that which we do not understand (نفهمه) and do not comprehend (نعقله). It is said: And these are representations of the aversion (نبوّ) of their hearts from perceiving what He invites them to and believing it, and the rejection (مَجِّ) of their ears toward it, and the refusal of their communication (مواصلتهم) and agreement (موافقتهم) with the Messenger صلى الله عليه وآله. فَاعْمَلْ ** So act according to your religion. إِنَّنا عامِلُونَ ** —we too are acting.' according to our religion.

(٦) قُلْ إِنَّما أَنَا بَشَرٌ مِثْلُكُمْ يُوحى إِلَيَّ أَنَّما إِلهُكُمْ إِلهٌ واحِدٌ ** (6) Say, 'I am only a human being like you, revealed to me is that your God is the One God.' I am not an angel nor a jinn (جِنّ). You cannot receive [revelation] from Him, nor do I call you to that which minds (العقول) and ears (الأسماع) reject. Rather, I only call you to Monotheism (التوحيد) and uprightness (الاستقامة) in action. فَاسْتَقِيمُوا ** (So be upright) in your deeds, turning towards Him, and seek His forgiveness for what you are upon. **وَوَيْلٌ لِلْمُشْرِكِينَ ** (Woe to the polytheists) due to the excess of their ignorance and their disregard for Allah.

(7) ** الَّذِينَ لا يُؤْتُونَ الزَّكاةَ ** (those who do not pay the zakat) due to their stinginess and lack of compassion towards the creation, وَهُمْ بِالْآخِرَةِ هُمْ كافِرُونَ. (and they are deniers of the Hereafter.)

In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Do you think that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, demanded the zakat of their wealth from the polytheists (المشركين) while they were associating partners with Him, where He says: وَوَيْلٌ لِلْمُشْرِكِينَ الَّذِينَ لا يُؤْتُونَ الزَّكاةَ وَهُمْ بِالْآخِرَةِ هُمْ كافِرُونَ ** (Woe to the polytheists, 7. those who do not pay the zakat, and they are deniers of the Hereafter.) It was said: May I be made your ransom, so explain its meaning to me. So He said: وَيْلٌ لِلْمُشْرِكِينَ ** (Woe to the polytheists) are those who associated partners with the First Imam (الإِمام الأوّل), and هُمْ ** (they) are deniers (كافِرُونَ) of the other Imams (الأئمّة الآخرين). Allah only called the servants to faith in Him, and when they believed in Allah and His Messenger, He made the obligatory duties (الفرائض) incumbent upon them.

I say: This narrative indicates what is established (التحقيق) in my view: that the disbelievers (الكفّار) are not obligated (غير مكلّفين) by the legal rulings (الاحكام الشرعيّة) as long as they remain in disbelief (الكفر).

And narrated from Ibn Abbas: meaning, they do not purify (يطهّرون) themselves from polytheism (الشّرك) through monotheism (التوحيد). And perhaps he only interpreted al-zakat (الزكاة) as purification (التطهير) because of what was mentioned.

(٨) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ لَهُمْ أَجْرٌ غَيْرُ مَمْنُونٍ ** (8) Indeed, those who have faith and do righteous deeds—there is a reward for them that will not be cut off. He will not remind them of it (as a favor). (٩) قُلْ أَإِنَّكُمْ لَتَكْفُرُونَ بِالَّذِي خَلَقَ الْأَرْضَ فِي يَوْمَيْنِ وَتَجْعَلُونَ لَهُ أَنْداداً ذلِكَ رَبُّ الْعالَمِينَ. (9) Say, 'Do you indeed disbelieve in Him who created the earth in two days, and set up equals to Him? That is the Lord of all the worlds.' (١٠) وَجَعَلَ فِيها رَواسِيَ مِنْ فَوْقِها وَبارَكَ فِيها ** (10) And He set in it firm mountains from above it, and blessed it, and multiplied its goodness وَقَدَّرَ فِيها أَقْواتَها فِي أَرْبَعَةِ أَيَّامٍ سَواءً لِلسَّائِلِينَ and determined therein its provisions in four days, equally for those who ask. Al-Qummi said: The meaning of يَوْمَيْنِ ** (two days) is two periods (وقتين): the beginning of creation and its completion. He said: وَبارَكَ فِيها وَقَدَّرَ فِيها أَقْواتَها ** (and blessed it and determined therein its provisions) means that they do not cease and they remain. فِي أَرْبَعَةِ أَيَّامٍ سَواءً ** (in four days, equally) means in four periods (أوقات), which are those in which Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, brings forth the provisions (أقوات) of the world—for humans, livestock, birds, creeping creatures of the earth, and the creation in the land and the sea, including fruits, plants, and trees, and whatever sustains all animals. These are spring (الربيع), summer (الصيف), autumn (الخريف), and winter (الشتاء). So, in winter (الشتاء), Allah sends the winds, the rains, the dews (الأنداء), and the mists (الطلول) from the sky, thereby fertilizing the earth and the trees, and it is a cold period. Then spring (الربيع) comes after it, which is a moderate period, hot and cold, wherein the trees bring forth fruit and the earth brings forth its vegetation, which is green and weak. Then the period of summer (الصيف) comes, which is hot, ripening the fruits and hardening the grains, which are the provisions (أقوات) of the world and all animals. Then the period of autumn (الخريف) comes after it, making it pleasant and cooling it. If the entire period were one single thing, the vegetation would not emerge from the earth, because if the entire period were spring (ربيعاً), the fruits would not ripen and the grains would not mature. And if it were all summer (صيفاً), everything on earth would be burned, and there would be no sustenance (معاش) or provision (قوت) for the animals. And if the entire period were autumn (خريفاً), and none of these periods preceded it, there would be nothing for the world to sustain itself with. So, Allah made these provisions (الأقوات) in four periods: winter (الشتاء), spring (الربيع), summer (الصيف), and autumn (الخريف). By means of this, the world subsists (قام), is balanced (استوى), and remains. And Allah named these periods days (ايّاماً) for those who ask (للسّائلين), meaning the needy (المحتاجين), because every needy person is an asker (سائل). And among the creation of Allah in the world, there are many animals who do not ask and are unable to do so, yet they are askers (سائلون) even if they do not ask. I say: This means that they are askers (سائلون) by the tongue of their state (بلسان الحال), which is clearer (افصح) and more eloquent (أبلغ) than the tongue of speech (لسان المقال). The interpretation of the end of the Ayah has already preceded in Surat al-A'raf. And it was recited سواءٍ (equally) with kasrah (بالجرّ).

(١١) ثُمَّ اسْتَوى إِلَى السَّماءِ ** (11) Then He turned to the sky It was said: meaning He intended towards it, derived from their saying, "He turned (اسْتَوى) to such-and-such a place," meaning He directed Himself with a direction that does not turn to anything else. And ثُمَّ (then) is for the distinction (تفاوت) between the two creations, not for temporal delay (التراخي في المدّة), since there was no duration (مدّة) before the creation of the heaven (السماء). وَهِيَ دُخانٌ ** while it was smoke, dark (ظلمانيّ). فَقالَ لَها وَلِلْأَرْضِ ائْتِيا طَوْعاً أَوْ كَرْهاً ** and said to it and to the earth, ‘Come willingly or unwillingly.’ whether you willed that or refused. قالَتا أَتَيْنا طائِعِينَ ** They said, ‘We come willingly.’ submissive by nature (بالذات). This is a representation (تمثيل) of the effect of His power upon them and their being essentially affected by it, through the command of the One Who is obeyed (المطاع) and the response of the obedient, willing subject (المطيع الطائع), like His saying: كُنْ فَيَكُونُ ** (Be, and it is). Or it is a type of inner speech (الكلام باطناً) without letter (حرف) or sound (صوت).

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ): Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him) was asked about whom Allah spoke to, who was neither from the jinn (الجنّ) nor from mankind (الانس). He said: The heavens and the earth, regarding His saying: ائْتِيا طَوْعاً أَوْ كَرْهاً قالَتا أَتَيْنا طائِعِينَ. (‘Come willingly or unwillingly.’ They said, ‘We come willingly.’)

(١٢) فَقَضاهُنَّ سَبْعَ سَماواتٍ ** (12) Then He determined them as seven heavens meaning, He created them with an innovative creation (خلقاً إبداعياً) فِي يَوْمَيْنِ ** in two days. Al-Qummi said: meaning, in two times (وقتين): a beginning (إبداء) and an end (انقضاء). وَأَوْحى فِي كُلِّ سَماءٍ أَمْرَها ** and He revealed the ordinance of each heaven in it. (meaning) its affair (شأنها) and what proceeds from it, by making it bear that, either by choice (اختياراً) or by nature (طبعاً). And it was said that He revealed His commands (أوامره) to its inhabitants (أهلها). And Al-Qummi said: this revelation (وحي) is a revelation of determination (تقدير) and management (تدبير). وَزَيَّنَّا السَّماءَ الدُّنْيا بِمَصابِيحَ ** and We adorned the lowest heaven with lamps (meaning) with the stars (بالنجوم) وَحِفْظاً ** and as a safeguard. from the eavesdropping devil (الشيطان المسترق) and all other afflictions (الآفات). In al-Ikmal, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "The stars are a security (أمان) for the inhabitants of the heaven, so if the stars depart, the inhabitants of the heaven depart. And My Household (أهل بيتي) are a security for the inhabitants of the earth, so if My Household depart, the inhabitants of the earth depart." ذلِكَ تَقْدِيرُ الْعَزِيزِ الْعَلِيمِ ** That is the determination of the All-mighty, the All-knowing. (meaning) the One Who is perfect (البالغ) in power (القدرة) and knowledge (العلم).

(١٣) ** فَإِنْ أَعْرَضُوا ** Should they turn away from faith after this clarification (البيان).

Al-Qummi (said): And they are Quraysh. And it is conjoined (معطوف) to His saying: فَأَعْرَضَ أَكْثَرُهُمْ فَهُمْ لا يَسْمَعُونَفَقُلْ أَنْذَرْتُكُمْ صاعِقَةً مِثْلَ صاعِقَةِ عادٍ وَثَمُودَ. (But most of them turned away, and they do not hear. Say: 'I warn you of a thunderbolt like the thunderbolt of 'Ad and Thamud.')

(١٤) إِذْ جاءَتْهُمُ الرُّسُلُ مِنْ بَيْنِ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِمْ ** (14) when the apostles came to them from before them and from behind them, meaning, from all their sides (جوانبهم), and they strove with them from every direction, or [it means] from the direction of this world (الدنيا) by warning them of what befell the disbelievers therein, and from the direction of the Hereafter (الآخرة) by cautioning them against what has been prepared for them therein, and [this refers to] those who were sent to them and those who were sent before. أَلَّا تَعْبُدُوا إِلَّا اللهَ قالُوا لَوْ شاءَ رَبُّنا ** that you shall not worship anyone except Allah. They said, 'Had our Lord wished, [meaning, had He wished] the sending of the Messengers (الرّسل), لَأَنْزَلَ مَلائِكَةً ** He would have sent down angels. [to convey] His message (برسالته). فَإِنَّا بِما أُرْسِلْتُمْ بِهِ ** So we indeed disbelieve in what you have been sent with.' according to your claim (على زعمكم). كافِرُونَ ** since you are humans like us, and you have no superiority (فضل) over us.

(١٥) فَأَمَّا عادٌ فَاسْتَكْبَرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ بِغَيْرِ الْحَقِ ** (15) Then as for 'Ad, they acted arrogantly in the land unjustly So they became mighty therein over its people without deserving it. وَقالُوا مَنْ أَشَدُّ مِنَّا قُوَّةً ** and they said, 'Who is greater than us in power?' They were deluded by their strength and their might (شوكتهم). It was said that part of their strength was that a man among them would pull out the rock (الصخرة) and uproot it with his hand. أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا أَنَّ اللهَ الَّذِي خَلَقَهُمْ هُوَ أَشَدُّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةً ** Did they not see that Allah, who created them, is greater than them in power? (Meaning: in) capability (قدرة). وَكانُوا بِآياتِنا يَجْحَدُونَ ** And they used to impugn Our signs. They knew that they were true (حقّ) yet they denied them.

(١٦) ** فَأَرْسَلْنا عَلَيْهِمْ رِيحاً صَرْصَراً. (16) So We unleashed upon them a freezing wind Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): al-Sarsar (الصرصر) is the cold (wind). فِي أَيَّامٍ نَحِساتٍ ** in ill-fated days, He said: (مياشيم). And it was recited with sukūn (بالسكون). لِنُذِيقَهُمْ عَذابَ الْخِزْيِ فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا وَلَعَذابُ الْآخِرَةِ أَخْزى وَهُمْ لا يُنْصَرُونَ that We might make them taste the punishment of disgrace in the life of the world, and the punishment of the Hereafter is surely more disgraceful, and they will not be helped. by repelling the punishment from them.

(١٧) وَأَمَّا ثَمُودُ فَهَدَيْناهُمْ ** (And as for Thamūd, We guided them) meaning, We made them submissive (فَذَلَّلْنَاهُمْ) to the truth by establishing proofs (بِنَصْبِ الْحُجَجِ) and sending Messengers (وَإِرْسَالِ الرُّسُلِ). فَاسْتَحَبُّوا الْعَمى عَلَى الْهُدى ** (but they preferred blindness to guidance) meaning, they chose error (الضلالة). عَلَى الْهُدى. (to guidance.)

In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): We made them know (عَرَّفْنَاهُمْ). فَاسْتَحَبُّوا الْعَمى عَلَى الْهُدى ** (but they preferred blindness to guidance). They know (هُمْ يَعْرِفُونَ).

And in al-I'tiqadat, narrated from him (peace be upon him): [We made them know] the obligation of obedience (وُجُوبُ الطَّاعَاتِ) and the prohibition of disobedience (وَتَحْرِيمُ الْمَعَاصِي), and they know (وَهُمْ يَعْرِفُونَ). فَأَخَذَتْهُمْ صاعِقَةُ الْعَذابِ الْهُونِ بِما كانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ. (So the thunderbolt of the humiliating punishment seized them because of what they used to earn.)

(١٨) وَنَجَّيْنَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَكانُوا يَتَّقُونَ. (18) But We delivered those who had faith and were Godwary.) (١٩) وَيَوْمَ يُحْشَرُ أَعْداءُ اللهِ إِلَى النَّارِ ** And it was read (وقرئ) with the nūn (بالنون) and the ḍammah on the shīn (وضمّ الشين) فَهُمْ يُوزَعُونَ ** (19) The day the enemies of Allah are mustered toward the Fire, they will be held back.) Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: meaning they come from every direction (ناحية). And from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "Their first ones are held back for their last ones (آخرهم)," meaning so that they may catch up with one another (ليتلاحقوا).

** حَتَّى إِذا ما جاؤُها ** (until, when they come to it) When they are present at it (اذا حضروها). And ما (mā) is extra (مزيدة) to emphasize the connection (اتّصال) of the witnessing (الشهادة) with the presence (بالحضور). **شَهِدَ عَلَيْهِمْ سَمْعُهُمْ وَأَبْصارُهُمْ وَجُلُودُهُمْ بِما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ ** (their hearing, their sight, and their skins will bear witness against them concerning what they used to do.) By Allah making them speak.

** وَقالُوا لِجُلُودِهِمْ لِمَ شَهِدْتُمْ عَلَيْنا قالُوا أَنْطَقَنَا اللهُ الَّذِي أَنْطَقَ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ وَهُوَ خَلَقَكُمْ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ ** (And they will say to their skins, 'Why did you bear witness against us?' They will say, 'Allah, who makes everything speak, has made us speak, and He created you the first time, and to Him you shall be brought back.') Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning the Day when their deeds are presented to them, and they deny them, saying, "We did not know anything." Then the angels who recorded their deeds against them bear witness against them.

Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: So they will say to Allah, "O Lord, these are Your angels bearing witness for You," then they swear by Allah that they did none of that. And this is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: يَوْمَ يَبْعَثُهُمُ اللهُ* ** (The day Allah raises them all,) the Mighty and Majestic جَمِيعاً فَيَحْلِفُونَ لَهُ كَما يَحْلِفُونَ لَكُمْ ** (they will swear to Him as they swear to you,) And they are those who usurped the right of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). It is then that Allah seals their tongues and makes their limbs speak. So the hearing bears witness to what it heard of that which Allah has forbidden, and the sight bears witness to what it looked at of that which Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has forbidden, and the two hands bear witness to what they took, and the two feet bear witness to what they strove towards of that which Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has forbidden, and the private part bears witness to what it committed of that which Allah has forbidden. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, makes their tongues speak, and they say: **لِجُلُودِهِمْ لِمَ شَهِدْتُمْ عَلَيْنا ** (to their skins, 'Why did you bear witness against us?') —the Ayah.

(٢٢) وَما كُنْتُمْ تَسْتَتِرُونَ ** (22) And you were not covering yourselves قال اي من الله He said: meaning, from Allah. أَنْ يَشْهَدَ عَلَيْكُمْ سَمْعُكُمْ وَلا أَبْصارُكُمْ وَلا جُلُودُكُمْ ** lest your hearing should bear witness against you, nor your sight, nor your skins. قال الجلود الفروج. He said: the skins (الجلود) are the private parts (الفروج). وفي الكافي عنه عليه السلام في هذه الآية قال : يعني بالجلود الفروج والأفخاذ And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, he said: He means by the skins (الجلود) the private parts (الفروج) and the thighs (الأفخاذ). وفي الفقيه عن أمير المؤمنين عليه السلام فيها قال : يعني بالجلود الفروج وَلكِنْ ظَنَنْتُمْ أَنَّ اللهَ لا يَعْلَمُ كَثِيراً مِمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ فلذلك اجترأتم على ما فعلتم And in al-Faqih, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) regarding it, he said: He means by the skins (الجلود) the private parts (الفروج). But you imagined that Allah does not know much of what you do. Therefore, you dared to commit what you did. وقيل معنى الآية كنتم تستترون النّاس عند ارتكاب الفواحش مخافة الفضاحة وما ظننتم انّ أعضاءكم تشهد عليكم فما استترتم عليها And it was said: The meaning of the verse is that you used to cover yourselves from people when committing immoral acts (الفواحش) for fear of disgrace, but you did not imagine that your limbs would bear witness against you, so you did not cover yourselves from them. وقيل بل معناه وما كنتم تتركون المعاصي حذراً ان يشهد عليكم جوارحكم بها لأنّكم ما تظنّون ذلك And it was said: Rather, its meaning is that you were not abandoning sins (المعاصي) out of caution that your limbs (جوارحكم) would bear witness against you concerning them, because you did not imagine that. وَلكِنْ ظَنَنْتُمْ أَنَّ اللهَ لا يَعْلَمُ كَثِيراً مِمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ ** But you imagined that Allah does not know much of what you do لجهلكم بالله فهان عليكم ارتكاب المعاصي لذلك. due to your ignorance of Allah, and thus the commission of sins (المعاصي) became easy for you because of that.

** وَذلِكُمْ ظَنُّكُمُ الَّذِي ظَنَنْتُمْ بِرَبِّكُمْ أَرْداكُمْ فَأَصْبَحْتُمْ مِنَ الْخاسِرِينَ ** (23) And that, your assumption which you assumed about your Lord, has ruined you, and you have become losers.

Since that which they were granted for seeking happiness in the two abodes (الدارين) became a cause for the misery of those who were brought down (to ruin).

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny) said: Verily, the last servant who is commanded to the Fire, when he is commanded to it, he turns around. Then the All-Compeller (الجبّار), majestic is His glory, says: "Return him." So they return him. He says to him: "Why did you turn to Me?" He says: "O Lord, this was not my assumption (ظنّي) about You." He says: "And what was your assumption about Me?" He says: "O Lord, my assumption about You was that You would forgive my sin and settle me in Your Paradise." He (the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: Then the All-Compeller (الجبّار) says: "O My angels! Nay, by My Might (عزّتي), My Majesty (جلالي), My Bounties (آلائي), My Loftiness (عُلوّي), and the elevation of My station (ارتفاع مكاني), this servant of Mine never assumed any good of Me for a single moment, ever. Had he assumed good of Me for a single moment, I would not have terrified him with the Fire. Permit his lie for him and admit him into Paradise." Then the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny) said: "There is no servant who assumes good of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, except that He is according to his assumption of Him. And that is His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: وَذلِكُمْ ظَنُّكُمُ الَّذِي ظَنَنْتُمْ بِرَبِّكُمْ أَرْداكُمْ فَأَصْبَحْتُمْ مِنَ الْخاسِرِينَ."

(٢٤) فَإِنْ يَصْبِرُوا فَالنَّارُ مَثْوىً لَهُمْ (24) If they endure, the Fire will be their abode, there is no escape (خلاص) for them from it. وَإِنْ يَسْتَعْتِبُوا and if they seek to please [Allah], they ask for al-ʿutbā (العتبى), which is the return to what they love, فَما هُمْ مِنَ الْمُعْتَبِينَ they will not be among those who are pleased. meaning, they will not be answered regarding that. And similar to it is the Almighty's saying, relating [their words]: أَجَزِعْنا أَمْ صَبَرْنا ما لَنا مِنْ مَحِيصٍ. Whether we are restless or patient, there is no escape for us.

(25) ** وَقَيَّضْنا ** And We decreed for them companions (قُرَناءَ). Al-Qummi said: meaning the devils (الشياطين) from among the jinn (الجنّ) and mankind (الإنس). ** فَزَيَّنُوا لَهُمْ ما بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ ** So they made fair-seeming to them what was before them, which is the affair of this world (الدنيا) and the pursuit of desires (الشهوات), ** وَما خَلْفَهُمْ ** and what was behind them, which is the affair of the Hereafter (الآخرة) and its denial. ** وَحَقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ الْقَوْلُ ** And the sentence became due against them, meaning the word of punishment (كلمة العذاب), ** فِي أُمَمٍ ** among nations, meaning within the totality of nations, ** قَدْ خَلَتْ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ مِنَ الْجِنِّ وَالْإِنْسِ ** that passed away before them from among the jinn and mankind, and they had performed deeds like their deeds. ** إِنَّهُمْ كانُوا خاسِرِينَ ** (25) And We assigned them companions, who made fair-seeming to them what was before them and what was behind them, and the sentence became due against them, among the nations of jinn and humans that passed away before them. Indeed, they were losers.

(٢٦) وَقالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لا تَسْمَعُوا لِهذَا الْقُرْآنِ وَالْغَوْا فِيهِ ** (26) The faithless say, 'Do not listen to this Quran, and talk frivolously during [its recitation], And oppose it with superstitions (الخرافات). Al-Qummi (said): And make it a mockery and frivolity. لَعَلَّكُمْ تَغْلِبُونَ ** so that you may overcome him (the Prophet) regarding its recitation. (٢٧) فَلَنُذِيقَنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا عَذاباً شَدِيداً وَلَنَجْزِيَنَّهُمْ أَسْوَأَ الَّذِي كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ ** (27) We will surely make the faithless taste a severe punishment, and We will surely requite them for the worst of what they used to do. The evil deeds (سيّئات) of their actions, and its like has preceded. (٢٨) ذلِكَ جَزاءُ أَعْداءِ اللهِ النَّارُ لَهُمْ فِيها دارُ الْخُلْدِ جَزاءً بِما كانُوا بِآياتِنا يَجْحَدُونَ ** (28) That is the requital of Allah's enemies—the Fire! For them therein will be the home of eternity, a requital for their gainsaying Our signs. They deny the truth (الحقّ). (٢٩) وَقالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا رَبَّنا أَرِنَا الَّذَيْنِ أَضَلَّانا مِنَ الْجِنِّ وَالْإِنْسِ ** (29) The faithless will say, 'Our Lord! Show us those of the jinn and humans who led us astray, The two devils (شيطاني) from the two types (jinn and humans) who incited error (الضلالة) and disobedience (العصيان). In al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): They mean Iblis, the chief of the devils (الأبالسة), and Qabil son of Adam, the first one who initiated disobedience (المعصية). And al-Qummi said that the Scholar (Imam) (peace be upon him) said: مِنَ الْجِنِ ** (of the jinn) refers to Iblis, who prevented the killing of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and led people astray through acts of disobedience (المعاصي), and who came to Abu Bakr after the death of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and pledged allegiance to him. And (the one) from the humans (الانس) is so-and-so (فلان). And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: "They are the two," then He said: "And so-and-so (فلان) was a devil (شيطاناً)." I say: Perhaps that is because the child of fornication (ولد الزنا) is created from the water (مائي) of the fornicator (الزّاني) and the devil (الشيطان) together. And in one narration: "They are the two, by Allah, they are the two" (said three times). And the word أرنا (show us) was read with takhfif (lightening the pronunciation). نَجْعَلْهُما تَحْتَ أَقْدامِنا ** that we may trample them under our feet, so that they may be among the lowest.' We will crush them (ندسّهما) in vengeance against them. لِيَكُونا مِنَ الْأَسْفَلِينَ ** in humiliation (ذلّا) and station (مكاناً).

(٣٠) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ قالُوا رَبُّنَا اللهُ acknowledging His Lordship (بربوبيّته) and affirming His Oneness (بوحدانيّته) ثُمَّ اسْتَقامُوا upon its requirement. Al-Qummi said: upon the wilayah (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), and what is similar in meaning will follow. And in Nahj al-Balaghah: "And I am speaking by the promise of Allah and His proof. Allah the Almighty said: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ قالُوا رَبُّنَا اللهُ ثُمَّ اسْتَقامُوا the Ayah. And you have said: رَبُّنَا اللهُ, so be steadfast upon His Book, upon the path (منهاج) of His command, and upon the righteous way (الطريقة الصالحة) of His worship. Then, do not deviate (تمرقوا) from it, do not innovate (تبتدعوا) in it, and do not oppose it, for those who deviate (اهل المروق) will be cut off from Him by Allah on the Day of Resurrection." تَتَنَزَّلُ عَلَيْهِمُ الْمَلائِكَةُ. In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) and al-Qummi: He said: at the time of death. أَلَّا تَخافُوا what you are approaching وَلا تَحْزَنُوا over what you have left behind وَأَبْشِرُوا بِالْجَنَّةِ الَّتِي كُنْتُمْ تُوعَدُونَ in the world (في الدنيا).

(30) Indeed, those who say, 'Our Lord is Allah,' then remain steadfast, the angels descend upon them, [saying:] 'Do not fear, nor grieve, and rejoice in the Garden that you were promised.'

(٣١) نَحْنُ أَوْلِياؤُكُمْ فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا ** (We are your friends in the life of the world) Al-Qummi said: We used to guard you from the devils (الشياطين). وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ ** (and in the Hereafter) He said: meaning, at the time of death (عند الموت). ** وَلَكُمْ فِيها ما تَشْتَهِي أَنْفُسُكُمْ وَلَكُمْ فِيها ما تَدَّعُونَ ** (and you shall have in it whatever your souls desire, and you shall have in it whatever you ask for) What you wish for/desire, from the supplication (الدّعاء) in the meaning of request (الطلب).

(٣٢) نُزُلاً مِنْ غَفُورٍ رَحِيمٍ. (32) as hospitality from an all-forgiving, all-merciful [Lord].

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: **اسْتَقامُوا ** (they stood straight/were steadfast) upon the Imams, one after the other.

In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked what al-istiqamah (الاستقامة) is. He said: "By Allah, it is what you are upon."

And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): نَحْنُ أَوْلِياؤُكُمْ فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا ** (We are your guardians in the life of the world), meaning: We guard you in the world and at the time of death فِي الْآخِرَةِ.** (and in the Hereafter).

And al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: No loyal follower (موالٍ) of ours who hates our enemies dies except that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, the Commander of the Faithful, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn عليهم السلام attend him. They see them and give them glad tidings. And if he is not a loyal follower (غير مُوالٍ), he sees them in a way that grieves him. The proof for this is the saying of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) to Harith al-Hamdani: (يا حار همدان من يمت يرني) "O Harith of Hamdan, whoever dies sees me," whether he is a believer (مؤمن) or a hypocrite (منافق), immediately.

In Tafsir al-Imam (peace be upon him), regarding the Almighty's saying يَظُنُّونَ أَنَّهُمْ مُلاقُوا رَبِّهِمْ ** (they are certain that they will encounter their Lord) from Surat al-Baqarah, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: The believer remains fearful of a bad outcome and is not certain of reaching the pleasure of Allah until the time of the extraction of his soul and the appearance of the Angel of Death to him. This is because the Angel of Death comes to the believer while he is in the severity of his illness and the great constriction of his chest due to the wealth he is leaving behind, and due to the disturbance of his affairs concerning his business partners and his family. The regrets remain in his soul, and he has been cut off before achieving his hopes, so he did not attain them. The Angel of Death says to him: "Why are you swallowing your sorrows (غصصك)?" He replies: "Due to the disturbance of my affairs and your cutting me off before my hopes." The Angel of Death says to him: "Does a rational person grieve over the loss of a counterfeit dirham (درهم زائف) when compensated with a thousand thousand times the world?" He replies: "No." The Angel of Death says: "Then look above you." He looks and sees the ranks of the Gardens (الجنان) and their palaces, which hopes fall short of reaching. The Angel of Death says: "Those are your dwellings, your blessings, your wealth, your family, and your dependents. And whoever among your family here and your righteous offspring are there with you. Are you not pleased with this as a substitute for what is here?" He replies: "Yes, by Allah." Then He says: "Look." He looks and sees Muhammad, Ali, and the pure ones (الطّيبين) from their progeny عليهم السلام in Illiyyin (علّيّين). He says: "Do you see them? These are your masters and your Imams. They are your companions (جُلّاسك) and your intimates (انّاسك) there. Are you not pleased with them as a substitute for what you are leaving here?" He replies: "Yes, by my Lord." That is what Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ قالُوا رَبُّنَا اللهُ ثُمَّ اسْتَقامُوا تَتَنَزَّلُ عَلَيْهِمُ الْمَلائِكَةُ أَلَّا تَخافُوا وَلا تَحْزَنُوا ** (Indeed, those who say, 'Our Lord is Allah,' and then stand straight, the angels descend upon them, [saying,] 'Do not fear, nor be grieved). [Do not fear] what is ahead of you from the horrors, for you have been spared them. And do not grieve over the offspring and family you leave behind, for this which you have witnessed in the Gardens is a substitute for them. وَأَبْشِرُوا بِالْجَنَّةِ الَّتِي كُنْتُمْ تُوعَدُونَ (and rejoice in the Garden that you were promised). These are your guardians (أولياؤكم), and these are your masters (ساداتكم), your intimates (انّاسكم), and your companions (جلّاسكم)."

In al-Basa'ir, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It was said to him: "We hear that the angels descend upon you." He said: "Yes, by Allah, they certainly descend upon us and tread upon our beds. Do you not read the Book of Allah, the Exalted: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ قالُوا رَبُّنَا اللهُ ** (Indeed, those who say, 'Our Lord is Allah,')—the Ayah."

In al-Khara'ij, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), regarding this Ayah, He said: "By Allah, sometimes we would place pillows (الوسائد) for them in our house." And He said: "They are kinder to our children than we are to them, and sometimes we would pick up from their down (زغبها)."

In al-Kafi, narrated from him, from his father, from his grandfather عليهم السلام, in the hadith about the Night of Decree (Laylat al-Qadr), He said: Ibn Abbas claimed that he was one of الَّذِينَ قالُوا رَبُّنَا اللهُ ثُمَّ اسْتَقامُوا ** (those who say, 'Our Lord is Allah,' and then stand straight). So I said to him: "Have you seen the angels informing you of their guardianship (ولايتها) over you in this world and the Hereafter, along with security from fear and grief?" He replied: "Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, says: إِنَّمَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ إِخْوَةٌ (The believers are indeed brothers), and the entire Ummah has entered into this." So I smiled, and then I said: "You have spoken the truth, O Ibn Abbas."

(٣٣) وَمَنْ أَحْسَنُ قَوْلاً (1) (33) And who has a better word مِمَّنْ دَعا إِلَى اللهِ ** to His worship (عبادته) وَعَمِلَ صالِحاً ** in what is between him and his Lord (ربّه) وَقالَ إِنَّنِي مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ. than someone who calls to Allah and acts righteously and says, 'I am one of the Muslims'?

Al-Ayyashi (العيّاشي) [narrated]: Indeed, it is concerning Ali (peace be upon him).

footnote (١) Its form is the form of interrogation (*الاستفهام*), but what is intended by it is negation (*النفي*). Its estimation (*تقديره*) is: And there is no one whose word is more firm than someone who calls to the obedience (*طاعة*) of Allah.

(٣٤) وَلا تَسْتَوِي الْحَسَنَةُ وَلَا السَّيِّئَةُ ** (34) Goodness and evil are not equal. [This refers] to the recompense (الجزاء) and the excellence of the outcome (حُسن العاقبة). The second لَا is added for emphasis of the negation (لتأكيد النفي). ادْفَعْ بِالَّتِي هِيَ أَحْسَنُ ** Repel [evil] with what is best. Repel the evil deed (السّيّئة) when it confronts you with that which is better (أحسن) than it, which is the good deed (الحسنة). This is based on the understanding that الأحسن (the best) means the absolute superior (الزائد مطلقاً), or the best of the good deeds (الحسنات) by which it is possible to repel it. فَإِذَا الَّذِي بَيْنَكَ وَبَيْنَهُ عَداوَةٌ كَأَنَّهُ وَلِيٌّ حَمِيمٌ Then, behold, he between whom and you was enmity will be as though he were a sympathetic friend. Meaning, if you do that, your hostile enemy (عدوّك المشاقّ) will become like the compassionate friend (الوليّ الشّفيق). Al-Qummi said: Repel the evil deed (سيّئة) of the one who wrongs you with your good deed (حسنتك), until الَّذِي بَيْنَكَ وَبَيْنَهُ عَداوَةٌ كَأَنَّهُ وَلِيٌّ حَمِيمٌ. (he between whom and you was enmity will be as though he were a sympathetic friend.)

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding His saying, the Almighty: وَلا تَسْتَوِي الْحَسَنَةُ وَلَا السَّيِّئَةُ ** (Goodness and evil are not equal). He said: The good deed (الحسنة) is al-Taqiyyah (التقية), and the evil deed (السيئة) is disclosure (الإذاعة). He said: That which is best (التي هي أحسن) is al-Taqiyyah (التقية).

(٣٥) وَما يُلَقَّاها ** (35) And none shall attain it And none shall attain this disposition (السجيّة), which is meeting evil with good (مقابلة الإساءة بالإحسان), إِلَّا الَّذِينَ صَبَرُوا ** (35) except those who are patient For it restrains the soul from vengeance (الانتقام).

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): إِلَّا الَّذِينَ صَبَرُوا ** (35) except those who are patient in the world (الدنيا) against harm (الأذى). وَما يُلَقَّاها إِلَّا ذُو حَظٍّ عَظِيمٍ ** (35) and none shall attain it except the man of great fortune of goodness (الخير) and perfection of the soul (كمال النفس).

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): وَما يُلَقَّاها إِلَّا ** (35) and none shall attain it except every possessor of great fortune (كلّ ذي حظّ عظيم).

(٣٦) ** وَإِمَّا يَنْزَغَنَّكَ مِنَ الشَّيْطانِ نَزْغٌ ** (36) (And if a temptation from Satan should tempt you) A prick (نخس) to which his whispering (وسوسته) was likened. then seek refuge in Allah from his evil and do not obey him. إِنَّهُ هُوَ السَّمِيعُ ** for your seeking refuge الْعَلِيمُ ** of your intention. Al-Qummi [said]: The address is directed to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, but the meaning is for the people.

(٣٧) ** وَمِنْ آياتِهِ اللَّيْلُ وَالنَّهارُ وَالشَّمْسُ وَالْقَمَرُ لا تَسْجُدُوا لِلشَّمْسِ وَلا لِلْقَمَرِ ** (37) Among His signs are the night and the day, and the sun and the moon. Do not prostrate to the sun, nor to the moon, because they are two created beings, commanded just like you. وَاسْجُدُوا لِلَّهِ الَّذِي خَلَقَهُنَّ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ إِيَّاهُ تَعْبُدُونَ but prostrate to Allah, who created them, if it is Him that you worship. For indeed, السجود is the most specific of the العبادات. Here is the place of prostration (موضع السجود), as narrated in al-Majma' from them.

(٣٨) فَإِنِ اسْتَكْبَرُوا ** (38) But if they are too arrogant [to worship Him], about compliance, فَالَّذِينَ عِنْدَ رَبِّكَ ** then those who are near your Lord from the angels يُسَبِّحُونَ لَهُ بِاللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهارِ ** glorify Him night and day, meaning constantly, وَهُمْ لا يَسْأَمُونَ ** and they do not tire. and they do not become weary. (٣٩) وَمِنْ آياتِهِ أَنَّكَ تَرَى الْأَرْضَ خاشِعَةً ** (39) And among His signs is that you see the earth dry, dry, subdued, borrowed from الخشوع (humility) in the sense of abasement (التذلّل). فَإِذا أَنْزَلْنا عَلَيْهَا الْماءَ اهْتَزَّتْ وَرَبَتْ ** but when We send down water upon it, it stirs and swells. it swelled with vegetation. إِنَّ الَّذِي أَحْياها ** Indeed, He who revives it after its death لَمُحْيِ الْمَوْتى إِنَّهُ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ.** is indeed the reviver of the dead. Indeed, He has power over all things.

(٤٠)** إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُلْحِدُونَ فِي آياتِنا لا يَخْفَوْنَ عَلَيْنا ** (40) Indeed those who commit deviation concerning Our signs are not hidden from Us.

they deviate from uprightness (الاستقامة) فِي آياتِنا by slandering (الطّعن), distorting (التحريف), interpreting with falsehood (التأويل بالباطل), and nullifying (الإِلغاء) them لا يَخْفَوْنَ from Us, so We shall recompense them for their deviation (إلحاد). And a discussion concerning this has already passed in the Sixth Introduction (المقدّمة السادسة) of this book.

Narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): **أَفَمَنْ يُلْقى فِي النَّارِ خَيْرٌ أَمْ مَنْ يَأْتِي آمِناً يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ اعْمَلُوا ما شِئْتُمْ ** Is he who is cast into the Fire better, or he who comes secure on the Day of Resurrection? Do whatever you like;

a severe threat (تهديد شديد) **إِنَّهُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ ** indeed He is watchful of what you do.

a warning of recompense (وعيد بالمجازاة).

(٤١) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِالذِّكْرِ لَمَّا جاءَهُمْ ** (41) Indeed those who disbelieve in the Reminder when it comes to them is an appositional substitute (بدل) for إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُلْحِدُونَ ** (Indeed those who deviate), or it is an independent clause (مستأنف), and the predicate (خبر) of inna is omitted (محذوف), or its predicate (خبره) is "those are called (أُولئِكَ يُنادَوْنَ)." Thus it was said. And Al-Qummi narrated from Al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding بِالذِّكْرِ : It means the Quran. وَإِنَّهُ لَكِتابٌ عَزِيزٌ. (And indeed it is a glorious Book.)

(٤٢) لا يَأْتِيهِ الْباطِلُ مِنْ بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ ** (42) falsehood does not approach it from before it He said: لا يَأْتِيهِ الْباطِلُ ** (falsehood does not approach it) from before the Torah (التوراة), nor from before the Gospel (الإنجيل) and the Psalms (الزّبور). وَلا مِنْ خَلْفِهِ ** (nor from behind it), meaning, no book comes after it to invalidate it. And in al-Majma', narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): There is no falsehood in its reporting concerning what has passed, nor is there falsehood in its reporting concerning what will be in the future; rather, all of its reports are in agreement with the things reported. تَنْزِيلٌ مِنْ حَكِيمٍ ** (a sending down from One who is Wise) meaning, Wise (حكيم). حَمِيدٍ ** (All-laudable) Whom every creature praises due to the favors (نعمه) that have become manifest upon it.

(٤٣) ما يُقالُ لَكَ إِلَّا ما قَدْ قِيلَ لِلرُّسُلِ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَذُو مَغْفِرَةٍ وَذُو عِقابٍ أَلِيمٍ ** (43) Nothing is said to you except what was said to the apostles before you. Your Lord is indeed the possessor of forgiveness and the possessor of a painful retribution. [Forgiveness] for His prophets, and [retribution] for their enemies. (٤٤) وَلَوْ جَعَلْناهُ قُرْآناً أَعْجَمِيًّا *It is said that this is a response to their saying: "Why was this Qur’an not revealed in the language of the non-Arabs (العجم*)?" لَقالُوا لَوْ لا فُصِّلَتْ آياتُهُ ** meaning: its verses were made clear in a tongue that we understand. ءَ أَعْجَمِيٌّ وَعَرَبِيٌ ** meaning: Is it non-Arabic speech (كلام أعجميّ) and an Arab addressee (مخاطب عربيّ)? Al-Qummi [said]: If this Qur’an were non-Arabic (أعجميّاً), they would say: "How shall we learn it, while our tongue is Arabic, and He has brought us a non-Arabic Qur’an?" So, He loved that it be revealed in their tongue. And concerning this, Allah said: وَما أَرْسَلْنا مِنْ رَسُولٍ إِلَّا بِلِسانِ قَوْمِهِ ** (And We did not send any apostle except with the language of his people). The term الأعجميّ (non-Arab/non-fluent) is applied to the one whose speech is not understood, and it is also applied to his speech. And it was read اعَجمِيّ (A'jamiyy) with the fathah on the ayn and the singular hamzah (توحيد الهمزة), indicating that it is attributed (منسوباً) to the non-Arabs (العجم). قُلْ هُوَ لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا هُدىً ** [Guidance] to the truth (الحقّ). وَشِفاءٌ ** from doubt (الشكّ) and suspicion (الشّبهة). وَالَّذِينَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ فِي آذانِهِمْ وَقْرٌ وَهُوَ عَلَيْهِمْ عَمًى ** due to their feigning deafness (لتصامّهم) from hearing it and their feigning blindness (وتعاميهم) from the signs (الآيات). أُولئِكَ يُنادَوْنَ مِنْ مَكانٍ بَعِيدٍ ** This is a similitude (تمثيل) for their lack of acceptance and their lack of listening to it, like one who is shouted at from a far distance. (44) Had We made it a non-Arabic Qur’an, they would have surely said, ‘Why have not its signs been articulated?’ What! Non-Arabic and Arabic? Say, ‘It is a guidance and a cure for those who have faith.’ But as for those who do not have faith, there is a deafness in their ears, and it is a blindness for them. They are like those who are called from a distant place.

** وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنا مُوسَى الْكِتابَ فَاخْتُلِفَ فِيهِ ** (And We certainly gave Moses the Book, whereupon they differed concerning it) just as there was difference concerning the Qur'an, and this is a consolation for the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام, he said: "They differed just as this Ummah differed concerning the Book, and they will differ concerning the Book that is with the Qā’im, which He will bring to them, until many people deny it, so He will present them and strike their necks."

وَلَوْ لا كَلِمَةٌ سَبَقَتْ مِنْ رَبِّكَ ** (And were it not for a word that went before from your Lord) concerning the respite, لَقُضِيَ بَيْنَهُمْ ** (it would have certainly been decided between them) by the uprooting of the deniers. وَإِنَّهُمْ لَفِي شَكٍّ مِنْهُ ** (and they are indeed in a dubious doubt concerning it) concerning the Qur'an, مُرِيبٍ ** (causing anxiety) causing agitation (موجب للاضطراب).

(٤٦) مَنْ عَمِلَ صالِحاً فَلِنَفْسِهِ ** — its benefit (نفعه). وَمَنْ أَساءَ فَعَلَيْها — its harm (ضرّه). ** وَما رَبُّكَ بِظَلَّامٍ لِلْعَبِيدِ ** (Whoever acts righteously, it is for his own sake, and whoever does evil, it is to his own detriment, and your Lord is not tyrannical to the servants.) — so that He would do to them what He is not entitled to do. (٤٧) إِلَيْهِ يُرَدُّ عِلْمُ السَّاعَةِ ** (To Him is referred the knowledge of the Hour.) — when He is asked about it, since none knows it except He. ** وَما تَخْرُجُ مِنْ ثَمَراتٍ مِنْ أَكْمامِها ** (No fruit emerges from its spathes) — from their containers (أوعيتها), which is the plural of كِمْ with a kasrah (بالكسر). And it was recited مِنْ ثَمَراتٍ (fruits) in the plural due to the variation of types. ** وَما تَحْمِلُ مِنْ أُنْثى وَلا تَضَعُ إِلَّا بِعِلْمِهِ ** (nor does any female conceive or deliver except by His knowledge.) — except that it is coupled with His knowledge, occurring according to its relation (تعلّقه) to Him. ** وَيَوْمَ يُنادِيهِمْ أَيْنَ شُرَكائِي ** (The day He calls out to them, ‘Where are My partners [whom you claimed]?’) — according to your claim (بزعمكم). Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) says: meaning what they used to worship besides Allah. ** قالُوا آذَنَّاكَ ** (They will say, ‘We inform You) — We have informed You (أعلمناك). ** ما مِنَّا مِنْ شَهِيدٍ ** (there is no witness among us [to that claim].’) — no one among us witnesses for them regarding partnership (بالشّركة), since we disavowed them when we witnessed the situation and the questioning for rebuke (للتّوبيخ); or, there is no one among us who sees them, because they have strayed from us.

(٤٨) وَضَلَّ عَنْهُمْ ما كانُوا يَدْعُونَ they used to worship مِنْ قَبْلُ وَظَنُّوا and they became certain ما لَهُمْ مِنْ مَحِيصٍ a refuge (مهرب). ** وَضَلَّ عَنْهُمْ ما كانُوا يَدْعُونَ مِن قَبْلُ وَظَنُّوا ما لَهُم مِّن مَّحِيصٍ ** (48) And what they used to invoke before will forsake them, and they will know that they have no escape. (٤٩) لا يَسْأَمُ الْإِنْسانُ مِنْ دُعاءِ الْخَيْرِ ** al-Qummi (said): meaning he does not tire (لا يملّ) nor become weary (ولا يعي) of praying for good for himself *وَإِنْ مَسَّهُ الشَّرُّ فَيَؤُسٌ قَنُوطٌ It was said (قيل): meaning despairing (يائس) of the comfort (روح) and relief (فرج) of Allah. ** لَّا يَسْأَمُ الْإِنسَانُ مِن دُعَاءِ الْخَيْرِ وَإِن مَّسَّهُ الشَّرُّ فَيَئُوسٌ قَنُوطٌ ** (49) Man is not weary of praying for good, but if an ill befalls him, he is despairing and hopeless. (٥٠) وَلَئِنْ أَذَقْناهُ رَحْمَةً مِنَّا مِنْ بَعْدِ ضَرَّاءَ مَسَّتْهُ by relieving him of it, he will surely say: 'This is my right (حقّي), which I deserve (استحقّه) due to the merit (الفضل) and action (العمل) that I possess, and it is always more fitting (أولى دائماً) and will not cease (لا يزول) وَما أَظُنُّ السَّاعَةَ قائِمَةً will occur (تقوم) وَلَئِنْ رُجِعْتُ إِلى رَبِّي إِنَّ لِي عِنْدَهُ لَلْحُسْنى meaning: and even if it were to occur based on mere conjecture (على التوهم), I would still have the best state (الحالة الحسنى) of honor (الكرامة) with Allah. This is due to his belief that the blessings of this world that afflicted him are due to a deserving right (لاستحقاق) that never leaves him (لا ينفك عنه). فَلَنُنَبِّئَنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِما عَمِلُوا We will surely recompense them (فلنجزينّهم) according to the reality of their deeds (بحقيقة أعمالهم), and We will surely aid them (ولينصرنّهم) contrary to what they believed regarding them وَلَنُذِيقَنَّهُمْ مِنْ عَذابٍ غَلِيظٍ which they cannot escape (لا يمكنهم التفصّي عنه*). ** وَلَئِنْ أَذَقْنَاهُ رَحْمَةً مِّنَّا مِن بَعْدِ ضَرَّاءَ مَسَّتْهُ لَيَقُولَنَّ هَذَا لِي وَمَا أَظُنُّ السَّاعَةَ قَائِمَةً وَلَئِن رُّجِعْتُ إِلَى رَبِّي إِنَّ لِي عِندَهُ لَلْحُسْنَى فَلَنُنَبِّئَنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِمَا عَمِلُوا وَلَنُذِيقَنَّهُم مِّنْ عَذَابٍ غَلِيظٍ ** (50) And if We let him taste a mercy from Us after a distress that has befallen him, he will surely say, ‘This is my due, and I do not think the Hour will ever be established. And if I am returned to my Lord, I will surely have the best with Him.’ But We will surely inform the faithless about what they have done, and We will surely make them taste a harsh punishment.

(٥١) وَإِذا أَنْعَمْنا عَلَى الْإِنْسانِ أَعْرَضَ ** (51) And when We bestow Our favour upon man, he turns away from gratitude وَنَأى بِجانِبِهِ ** and withdraws to his side, and deviates from it, and goes away with his soul, and distances himself from it entirely, out of arrogance. And al-jānib (الجانب) is a metaphor for the soul (النفس), like al-janb (الجنب) in His saying: فِي جَنْبِ اللهِ concerning Allah. وَإِذا مَسَّهُ الشَّرُّ ** And when evil touches him, such as poverty, illness, and hardship, then he is one of wide/extensive supplication (دُعاءٍ عَرِيضٍ), (meaning) much (كثير).

(٥٢) قُلْ أَرَأَيْتُمْ ** (Say, 'Tell me) Inform me, if it was from Allah—meaning the Quran—ثُمَّ كَفَرْتُمْ بِهِ ** (and then you disbelieved in it) without reflection or following evidence, مَنْ أَضَلُّ مِمَّنْ هُوَ فِي شِقاقٍ بَعِيدٍ ** (who is more astray than someone who is in extreme dissension?') Who is more astray than you? So He placed the relative pronoun (الموصول) in the place of the personal pronoun (الضمير), as an explanation of their condition and a justification for their increased deviation.

(٥٣) سَنُرِيهِمْ آياتِنا فِي الْآفاقِ وَفِي أَنْفُسِهِمْ حَتَّى يَتَبَيَّنَ لَهُمْ أَنَّهُ الْحَقُ ** (53) We shall show them Our signs in the horizons and in their own souls until it becomes clear to them that He is the Truth. It was said that We shall show them means Our proofs (حججنا) and Our evidences (دلائلنا) concerning that to which We call them, namely, Monotheism (التوحيد) and what follows it, in the horizons (آفاق) of the world, the regions (أقطار) of the heaven and the earth, the sun, the moon, the stars, the mountains, the seas, the trees, and the beasts; and in their own souls (وَفِي أَنْفُسِهِمْ), and what they contain of the subtleties of creation (لطائف الصنعة) and the deposits of wisdom (ودايع الحكمة), until it becomes clear to them that He is the Truth (أَنَّهُ الْحَقُ). أقولُ : I say: These people seek evidence for the Creator (الصانع) through the created things (الصنائع), as is the custom (داب) of the average people (المتوسطين) who are not satisfied with mere imitation (محض التقليد) and consider themselves above that. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: In the horizons (فِي الْآفاقِ) means the eclipses (الكسوف), the earthquakes (الزّلازل), and the signs (الآيات) that occur in the heaven. As for in their own souls (فِي أَنْفُسِهِمْ), it is sometimes through hunger, sometimes through thirst, sometimes one is satiated, sometimes one is quenched, sometimes one is sick, sometimes one is healthy, sometimes one is rich, sometimes one is poor, sometimes one is pleased, sometimes one is angry, sometimes one is afraid, and sometimes one is secure. This is part of the greatness of Allah's evidence (دلالة) for Monotheism (التوحيد). The poet said: And in everything there is a sign for Him, indicating that He is One. I say: This is a specification of the signs (الآيات) to some of them, which suits the understanding (افهام) of the common people (العوام). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: We show them in their own souls (فِي أَنْفُسِهِمْ) the transformation (المسخ), and We show them in the horizons (فِي الْآفاقِ) the disruption (انتقاض) of the horizons against them. Thus, they see the power of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, in their own souls and in the horizons. He was asked about until it becomes clear to them that He is the Truth (حَتَّى يَتَبَيَّنَ لَهُمْ أَنَّهُ الْحَقُ). He said: The emergence of the Qā’im (خروج القائم) (peace be upon him) is the Truth (الحقّ) from Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, which creation must inevitably see. And in a narration: sinking (خسف), transformation (مسخ), and hurling (قذف). He was asked about until it becomes clear (حَتَّى يَتَبَيَّنَ). He said: Leave that; that is the rising of the Qā’im (قيام القائم) (peace be upon him). In Irshad al-Mufid, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him), he said: The tribulations (الفتن) are in the horizons (آفاق) of the earth, and the transformation (المسخ) is among the enemies of the Truth (اعداء الحقّ). أقولُ : I say: It is as if he (peace be upon him) meant that this will only occur during the Return (الرجعة) and upon the emergence of the Qā’im (peace be upon him), where they will see such wonders (العجائب) and marvels (الغرائب) in the horizons (الآفاق) and in the souls (الأنفس) that it will become clear to them that the Imamate (الإمامة), the Guardianship (الولاية), and the emergence of the Imam are the Truth (حقّ). This is for the deniers (الجاحدين). ** أَوَلَمْ يَكْفِ بِرَبِّكَ أَنَّهُ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ شَهِيدٌ ** (54) Does it not suffice concerning your Lord that He is witness to everything? meaning: Does not the testimony (شهادة) of your Lord concerning everything suffice you as evidence (دليلاً) for Him? أقولُ : I say: This is for the elite (الخواص) who seek evidence for Allah through Allah, and for this reason, He singled them out with this address (الخطاب). In Misbah al-Shari’ah, al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: Servitude (العبوديّة) is a jewel whose essence (كنهها) is Lordship (الرّبوبيّة). Thus, whatever is lost from servitude is found in Lordship, and whatever is hidden from Lordship is attained in servitude. Allah the Almighty said: سَنُرِيهِمْ آياتِنا فِي الْآفاقِ (We shall show them Our signs in the horizons) up to His saying: شَهِيدٌ (witness), meaning: He is present (موجود) in your absence (غيبتك) and your presence (حضرتك).

(٥٤) أَلا إِنَّهُمْ فِي مِرْيَةٍ ** (54) Look! They are indeed in doubt (They are in) doubt concerning the meeting with their Lord, regarding the Resurrection (البعث) and the Recompense (الجزاء). أَلا إِنَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ مُحِيطٌ ** Look! He indeed comprehends all things. Knowing it, powerful over it; nothing escapes Him. And its interpretation (تأويله) is derived from what is in al-Misbah.

( In Thawab al-A'mal )

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Ha Mim al-Sajdah (حم السّجدة) (Surah Fussilat), it will be a light for him on the Day of Resurrection as far as his sight extends, and a joy, and he will live in this world praised and envied (مغبوطاً).

And in al-Khisal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Indeed, the al-Aza'im (العزائم) are four, and he counted this Surah among them, as previously mentioned regarding Alif Lam Mim al-Sajdah (الم السجدة).
